name: ETH 1h Value Recovery Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: price recovers short-term value after a flush, confirmation improves across bars, and exposure is built in stages instead of one-shot all-in timing
thesis: Cycle 74 explicitly asks for scale-in around value recovery when confirmation improves across bars instead of one-shot all-in timing. ETH 1h is the best fit because the regime is transition-with-chop and should produce repeated value-loss/value-recovery sequences. The edge is not guessing the first bounce; it is adding only as recovery proves itself.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 1h value-recovery sequences still undertrade or if staged adds simply average into failed bounces, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes away from value, reclaims value, confirms that reclaim across successive bars, and resumes away from the recovery zone.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition markets often punish one-shot timing but reward building into improving confirmation. Scaling in after value recovery should improve location versus full-size first-touch entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 1h is the best fit because it is the primary lane, active enough for repeated recovery sequences, and less structurally noisy than AXS.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The staged entries may still build into weak mean-reversion bounces that fail before real continuation appears.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trade count is good but expectancy is weak, reduce second-add size and take the first partial sooner before changing entry logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Frequent recovery attempts, many modest first-target wins, and fewer larger moves when recovery becomes true continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 1h clears density and staged entries materially outperform one-shot recovery timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-20 trades, or repeated scale-ins that turn into averaging losers.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: low on 1h because value-recovery events are common enough

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit primary lane for staged value recovery in a transition regime.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 1h shows enough value-recovery sequences with scale-in improving net expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h staged entries mostly build into failed bounces.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate-to-high participation with many modest recovery continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same value-recovery scale-in works on a similar major with different noise characteristics.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h keeps density and cleaner recovery sequencing.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h makes the setup too blunt or too flat.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Slightly fewer but cleaner recovery continuations than ETH.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the staged recovery logic survives a noisier lane without collapsing.
validation_basket.continue_if: AXS 1h preserves the same recovery grammar with tolerable volatility.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: AXS 1h turns the staged logic into overtrading noise.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher variance, faster recovery legs than ETH.

long: Initial 60% size on first close back above VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes >= EMA_20 and low stays >= VWAP - 0.08 * ATR_14
short: Initial 60% size on first close back below VWAP
Add 40% only if next bar closes <= EMA_20 and high stays <= VWAP + 0.08 * ATR_14

long: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 below blended entry
short: Stop loss: 0.82 * ATR_14 above blended entry
